lead.title: Trump-Xi Beijing Summit Opens Under Iran Shadow
lead.standfirst: Trump arrived in Beijing on May 12 for a state visit whose formal agenda — tariffs, rare earths, Taiwan, AI — has been crowded by an Iran ceasefire Trump himself called 'on massive life support,' with Xi holding more diplomatic leverage on Tehran than the White House had planned for.

secondary.title: Starmer Loses Four Junior Ministers as Leadership Threshold Looms
secondary.standfirst: Four junior ministers resigned May 12, with at least 83 Labour MPs publicly calling for Starmer to go — within striking distance of the 81-MP threshold to trigger a formal contest — as the State Opening of Parliament proceeds on May 13.

secondary.title: NBA Semifinals: Knicks and Thunder Sweep In; Spurs Take 3-2 Lead
secondary.standfirst: Three of four conference semifinals series effectively decided: Knicks and Thunder sweep into the conference finals, Spurs lead Timberwolves 3-2 with Wembanyama dominant, and Cavs-Pistons stays level entering Game 5 on May 13.

secondary.title: NBA Semifinals: Cavs and Spurs Up 3-2; Game 6s Friday
secondary.standfirst: Cleveland survived overtime in Detroit, San Antonio rolled at home, and both Game 6s land Friday with the Knicks and Thunder already waiting in the Conference Finals.

further_reading.title: April CPI Reaccelerates to 3.8% as Energy Shock Hits Headline
further_reading.standfirst: Headline CPI rose to 3.8% in April — the highest since May 2023 — driven by a 17.9% annual energy surge, but core inflation also reaccelerated to 2.8%, complicating the Fed's three-meeting hold and the White House's push for cuts.

further_reading.title: Victory Day Ceasefire Expires; Putin Floats Third-Country Meeting
further_reading.standfirst: The US-brokered three-day Victory Day ceasefire expired May 11 with mutual violation claims, even as the 1,000-for-1,000 prisoner swap proceeded and Putin offered, conditionally, to meet Zelenskyy in a third country once a long-term treaty is in place.

further_reading.title: Warsh Confirmed as Fed Chair in Closest Vote on Record
further_reading.standfirst: The Senate's 54-45 confirmation of Kevin Warsh as the 11th modern-era Fed chair is the narrowest such margin ever, and the FOMC he inherits is split four ways heading into the June meeting.

further_reading.title: PPI Hits 6% as Pipeline Inflation Builds Into Warsh's First Meeting
further_reading.standfirst: Wednesday's PPI release at 6.0% year-on-year — the hottest since December 2022 — frames April's CPI shock as a pipeline problem and removes any remaining 2026 rate-cut pricing from futures markets.

further_reading.title: Trump-Xi Open at the Great Hall; Deliverables Narrow as Truce Hangs
further_reading.standfirst: The Beijing summit opened Thursday with the largest US business delegation in nearly a decade, but the October trade truce extension is unconfirmed and the substantive agenda has narrowed to soybeans, Boeing and two new bilateral boards.

further_reading.title: House Discharge Petition Forces Ukraine Aid and Sanctions Vote Over Trump
further_reading.standfirst: Independent Kevin Kiley delivered the 218th signature Wednesday on a discharge petition that bypasses Speaker Johnson and the President to force a June floor vote on Ukraine aid, lend-lease, sanctions, and a 500% tariff on Russian goods.

further_reading.title: Cloudflare Cuts 1,100 Jobs at Record Revenue
further_reading.standfirst: Cloudflare announced its first mass layoff in sixteen years on May 7 — 1,100 jobs eliminated alongside record quarterly revenue — and explicitly attributed the cut to internal AI agent adoption. The move is the clearest disclosed case yet of AI displacement at a growing company.

further_reading.title: Wall Street's AI Chip Rotation
further_reading.standfirst: AMD, Intel, and Micron have outrun Nvidia by orders of magnitude year-to-date as Wall Street rotates AI-hardware capital from training GPUs to inference-era CPUs and memory. Nvidia's counter-move is a 0 billion equity-investment campaign across its own customer base.
